---
title: "Mathematical and Search Substrates"
chapter_id: "mathematical-and-search-substrates"
part_id: "routing-compression-representation-substrates"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "genesiscode"
  - "temporal_coil_research"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "treellm"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "coilmoecot"
  - "circle_calculus_core"
  - "circle_ai_architectures"
  - "proof_carrying_circular_computation"
  - "theseus_circle_transfer"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Source notes exist for all assigned sources, but claim-level source mapping remains incomplete."
  - "The substrate_adoption_record fixture validates schema/example consistency only; no substrate A/B run, representation-efficiency benchmark, CoilMoECOT benchmark, or local Circle build has been run."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

# Mathematical and Search Substrates

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `mathematical-and-search-substrates` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Representation, and Substrates |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_architectures`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation`, `theseus_circle_transfer` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`; supporting: `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_architectures`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation`, `theseus_circle_transfer`; connector/recovery: `coilmoecot` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_architectures`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation`, `theseus_circle_transfer`; raw cache: `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot` |
| Test state | `substrate_adoption_record.valid.json` passes repository-level protocol fixture validation; baseline comparison, representation-efficiency, and falsification tests remain planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter treats mathematical and search substrates as optional specialist candidates. It does not claim that coils, calculi, geometric search, or cyclic compute improve model quality, compression, or runtime without baselines and artifacts.

## Problem

The source corpus contains many potentially useful substrates: semantic IR, small deterministic kernels, temporal coils, cyclic address laws, proof-carrying receipts, TreeLLM-style semantic graphs, and routed specialist lanes. The book needs a place for them, but not a permission slip for overclaiming.

The problem is adoption discipline. A substrate can be mathematically clean and still useless for a workload. A proof can establish address safety while saying nothing about speed. An experiment plan can be well designed while producing no positive result. The stack must keep exploratory substrates available without making them core until evidence warrants it.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Novel substrates can become authority theater if they are not tied to baselines, adoption gates, and falsification criteria. Opaque math can be treated as proof. Single-seed experiments can be treated as discovery. A structural theorem can be laundered into a performance claim. A specialist lane can become load-bearing before its readiness gates exist.

GenesisCode supplies the small-kernel and obligation discipline. Temporal Coil Research supplies manifest-driven A/B testing. Circle Calculus supplies proof-status boundaries. The Theseus transfer lane supplies the rule that deterministic structural fixtures are not model-quality evidence.

## Core Claim

[mathematical-and-search-substrates.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Coils, calculi, and geometric search belong in the stack as optional specialist substrates until tests show where they improve search, routing, or compression.

The claim remains at `argument` support. The assigned sources support discussion of substrate candidates, proof boundaries, manifests, A/B variants, and adoption discipline. They do not promote any substrate to core architecture.

## Mechanism

The mechanism is a Substrate Adoption Record.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Candidate substrate"] --> B["Expected advantage"]
  B --> C["Ordinary baselines"]
  C --> D["Falsification condition"]
  D --> E["Experiment or proof boundary"]
  E --> F{"Evidence sufficient?"}
  F -- "yes" --> G["Canary specialist route"]
  F -- "no" --> H["Remain exploratory / residual"]
  G --> I["Readiness gate"]
  H --> I
```

The record should separate:

- Substrate identity and intended use.
- Expected advantage.
- Required baselines and negative controls.
- Proof or structural facts, if any.
- Workload, metric, script, and report requirements.
- Falsification condition.
- Adoption decision and residuals.

This lets the architecture keep a library of promising substrates without turning the library into a mythology.

## Interfaces

The interface is the Substrate Adoption Record.

Minimum fields:

- `substrate_id`
- `substrate_kind`
- `intended_use`
- `expected_advantage`
- `baseline_refs`
- `negative_controls`
- `proof_boundary`
- `experiment_requirements`
- `falsification_condition`
- `adoption_state`
- `residuals`
- `evidence_refs`

Routing treats a candidate substrate as a specialist. Compression tests representation efficiency. Evidence compares against baselines. Proof envelopes preserve theorem status. Readiness gates decide whether a substrate is exploratory, canary, qualified, or retired.

## Invariants

- Exploratory claims stay exploratory.
- Baselines are recorded.
- Failed hypotheses remain visible.
- Proof of a structural property is not proof of task quality.
- A substrate without passing evidence remains non-core.

The adoption invariant is intentionally conservative: the default state is optional. A substrate earns broader use through bounded evidence, not through elegance.

## Failure modes

- Performance overclaiming.
- Opaque math treated as proof.
- Adoption without regression tests.
- Single-seed or confounded experiments promoted as results.
- Structural fixtures mistaken for learned-model benchmarks.

Each failure should produce a downgrade, residual, or blocked promotion. Negative results are useful; hidden negative results are architectural debt.

## Minimal implementation

The smallest useful implementation is a substrate adoption schema and fixture. The current fixture records the candidate, intended use, advantage, baselines, negative controls, proof boundary, experiment requirements, falsification condition, adoption state, residuals, and evidence references.

Passing the fixture does not prove a substrate. It prevents the book from describing adoption without the fields that adoption requires.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Substrate adoption record fixture validation | Validate that a candidate substrate records intended use, expected advantage, baselines, negative controls, proof boundary, experiment requirements, falsification condition, adoption state, residuals, and evidence references. | implemented; passing via `python3 scripts/validate_protocol_examples.py` |
| Baseline comparison test | Check that adoption records name ordinary baselines before quality claims. | planned; not run |
| Representation efficiency test | Check whether a substrate improves a bounded representation task against a baseline. | planned; not run |
| Falsification review | Check that the record names what would count against adoption. | planned; not run |

The implemented row validates fixture/schema consistency only. The remaining rows require concrete workloads, baselines, negative controls, and run artifacts; they are not reported substrate results. When a substrate test is implemented, this chapter should link to the command, fixture, environment notes, workload, and result summary, and Appendix E should be regenerated or updated accordingly.

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:substrates.search.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.SearchSubstrates` | A substrate adoption record includes baseline, measured target, and falsification criterion. | planned |
| `lean:substrates.search.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.SearchSubstrates` | A substrate without passing evidence remains non-core. | planned |

These Lean hooks remain planned. The schema can require adoption evidence fields, but it does not prove a substrate improves search, routing, compression, or representation. The first proof target should model the promotion gate: without passing evidence, a substrate remains non-core.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | executable_specification | Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `temporal_coil_research` | Temporal Coil Research | mathematical_search_substrate | Manifest-driven A/B process for temporal-coil integration in language model training. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | planning_semantic_ir | Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | semantic_representation | Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | compute_fidelity_constraints | Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `coilmoecot` | CoilMoECOT Whitepaper v2.0 | mathematical_search_substrate | Prime-temporal coil networks as governed specialist cores in MoECOT runtime. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `circle_calculus_core` | Circle Calculus | proof_carrying_mathematical_substrate | Proof-carrying finite cyclic mathematics project with Lean proofs, Python reference models, Rust utilities, theorem manifests, papers, and Quarto living book. | source note available |
| `circle_ai_architectures` | Circle AI Architectures | cyclic_ai_architecture | Disciplined Circle AI thesis: use phase, recurrence, rotation, sparse cyclic mixing, circular memory, harmonic transforms, or geometry-aware structure only where the structure is real and baselines support it. | source note available |
| `proof_carrying_circular_computation` | Proof-Carrying Circular Computation | proof_carrying_compute_substrate | CoilIR-style path from circle/coil expressions to dictionary-recognized cyclic structure, Lean-proved rewrite/address transformations, backend selection, and benchmark validation. | source note available |
| `theseus_circle_transfer` | Theseus Circle Calculus Transfer Lane | proof_contract_transfer | Report-only bridge from Circle finite fixtures into private Theseus benchmark design with explicit quality/runtime/memory/transfer/failure-case claim boundaries. | source note available |

The crosswalk keeps optional substrates optional. `genesiscode` supplies small-kernel and obligation discipline, `temporal_coil_research` supplies manifest-driven A/B discipline, `coilmoecot` stays a readable design source rather than benchmark evidence, and the Circle sources supply proof-boundary language. None of the assigned sources promotes a substrate without baselines, controls, and adoption evidence.

## Summary

Mathematical substrates are valuable when they become disciplined specialists. They should carry baselines, proof boundaries, falsification conditions, evidence requirements, and readiness states.

The stack can explore coils, calculi, semantic graphs, and cyclic compute without making them core prematurely. Optionality is not weakness; it is how the architecture keeps evidence in charge.
